summary: First iteration testing of KIEMA Editing App (Gemini Nano Banana image gen + Sora 2 validation). 9/9 backend pytest tests passed (health, auth gate, image gen with & without reference, gallery list+filter, delete, video duration validation). Frontend Playwright tests passed end-to-end: landing renders correct h1, /studio redirects when unauth, session cookie injection unlocks /studio, prompt input + Generate produces a real 4K image in canvas (~14s), Gallery shows item with All/Images/Videos filters working correctly.

issues: Generate button label is partly clipped behind the toast (cosmetic only; doesn't affect functionality)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/auth/session uses session_token from Emergent oauth response and inserts duplicate user_sessions rows on each call. Consider upsert on session_token to avoid duplicates if same session_id is exchanged twice.

server.py: Image generation persists full base64 data URL inside MongoDB document; for users with many generations this will bloat the DB and slow /api/generations. Consider storing image bytes on disk (like videos) and referencing by id.
server.py: /api/generations response could be very large (200 items x base64 images). Consider returning a thumbnail or omitting image_data_url from list endpoint and exposing a per-item GET.
server.py is 367 lines — still maintainable but starting to mix concerns (auth, generation, media). Acceptable for MVP.
test_credentials: Seeded via mongosh per /app/auth_testing.md. Token used: TEST_session_1782463465417 (backend) / TEST_session_fe_1782463521712 (frontend). Both cleaned up after run.
seed_data_creation: Inserted 2 TEST_ users + sessions via mongosh; cleaned up all TEST_ data (users, sessions, generations) at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_kiema_api.py reads KIEMA_SESSION_TOKEN env var. Seed user+session via mongosh (see /app/auth_testing.md) and pass token via env. Image gen takes ~10-20s and consumes real Emergent LLM credits — avoid looping. Video generation (Sora 2) is verified only via 400 on invalid duration; do NOT actually call it. Frontend uses cookie-based session_token with samesite=none/secure/httponly; inject via page.context.add_cookies as in auth_testing.md.
